name: @chbrain/khai-engine-org
description: khai engine content, the organisational-culture domain: how an organisation operates -- its competing-values stance and the processes it runs.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Organisational culture as process: how an organisation operates -- its competing-values stance (clan, adhocracy, market, hierarchy) and the four processes it runs (collaborate, create, compete, control).

card.wire: Attaches at Process. The anchor (process_operate_org.md) declares how an organisation operates: a stance on two axes (flexibility vs stability, internal vs external focus) that sets which of four processes it reaches for. The stance colours every persona who acts inside the organisation.
card.issue: Four stance positions a context may hold -- clan, adhocracy, market, hierarchy (the Competing Values quadrants) -- each paired with the process it runs: collaborate, create, compete, control. A real organisation mixes them; the dominant quadrant sets the default and the others fire as the scene demands.
card.require: Two altitudes hold the engine. The law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor, so the organisation's stance is read where personas act inside it. The link: the organisational context links its dominant stance, and a persona acting within it links the process the moment runs.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared and a real stance or process is linked. Whether the stance is true to the organisation and the scene is semantic, taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite.
card.setup: Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor. Then give the organisational context its stance and let a persona link the process the moment runs. Read it in how the room coordinates, not as an org-chart label.
